session_summary: This session opened by tasking the AI with a Shimodaira positioning verdict (is the net tr(RIV) correction precedented, is the MAR (A)+(C) term genuinely beyond their framework), then moved into a plan-mode sequencing of the MAR derivation work, a reproducible phase-8 evidence run, and the general-MVN (A)+(C) write-up. Marcus's role was to set the agenda with a precise multi-part gate question, impose reproducibility discipline on temporary scripts, supply literature-verification routing (semantic scholar / pubmed / crossref), make compute and scope calls (R=40k run, option A), and spin out a side idea for a derivation-audit skill. He closed by directing a commit and a handoff prompt for the next session.

coded_turns.quote: GATE TASK — do this first, fresh: read literature/shimodairaInformationCriterionModel2017.md in full
coded_turns.note: Sets the agenda: a three-part positioning verdict gating novel-vs-precedented; holds all derivation until settled.

coded_turns.quote: aren't we also showing how this generalizes toe multiple imp;tuation and not just EM?
coded_turns.note: Pushes back on the novelty framing: the MI/posterior-draw generalization beyond EM is part of the contribution.

coded_turns.quote: I rather take a "knowledge accumulates and that shifts the document" than "lets get htis right in one shot"
coded_turns.note: Calls for plan mode and a sequential plan, accepting iterative rework over one-shot correctness.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: plan-mode sequencing of the MAR derivation

coded_turns.quote: But also be sure to prioritize reproducibility
coded_turns.note: Imposes reproducibility as a planning priority.

coded_turns.quote: if it is going to be used as evidence of soemthing permanent then it needs to be reproducible
coded_turns.note: Tightens the rule: temporary scripts fine, but permanent evidence must be reproducible.

coded_turns.quote:
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Background command "Render derivation.qmd to verify Phase 2 edits" completed (exit code 0)</summary>
coded_turns.note: Task notification surfacing a clean render; passive monitoring of a background verification job.

coded_turns.quote: Give me the author, title, and doi verified by crossref so I can quickly integrate these into zotero
coded_turns.note: Demands externally verified citation metadata (crossref) before integrating references; citation discipline.

coded_turns.quote: verified by semantic scholar tool in litrev so I can quickly integrate these into zotero
coded_turns.note: Reroutes verification to the litrev semantic scholar tool; authoritative source over memory.

coded_turns.quote: Verify with pubmed
coded_turns.note: Cross-checks DOIs against a second authoritative source; redundancy on citation metadata.

coded_turns.quote: All three pdfs are not in zotero and need to migrate to google drive
coded_turns.note: Reports state of the acquisition pipeline: PDFs absent, must migrate to drive before lit-sync.

coded_turns.quote: Alright -- it has been indexed. The BBT has been exported.
coded_turns.note: Confirms he completed the indexing and BBT export step in the acquisition pipeline.

coded_turns.quote: <summary>Background command "Smoke-test phase-8 Term-A MAR module (R=100)" completed (exit code 0)</summary>
coded_turns.note: Task notification: phase-8 module smoke test passed; monitoring of the reproducible evidence build.

coded_turns.quote: <summary>Background command "Smoke-test phase-8 module with full Rscript path" completed (exit code 0)</summary>
coded_turns.note: Task notification: corrected smoke run (full R path, no pipe) passed after a false-positive was caught.

coded_turns.quote: <summary>Background command "Calibrate phase-8 wall-clock at R=2000 (extrapolate to high R)" completed (exit code 0)</summary>
coded_turns.note: Task notification: wall-clock calibration run completed, feeding the R-choice decision.

coded_turns.quote: <summary>Background command "Calibrate phase-8 wall-clock at R=2000 (clean)" completed (exit code 0)</summary>
coded_turns.note: Task notification: clean calibration run completed, doubling as the R=2000 phase-8 cache.

coded_turns.quote: and this is with 20 cores?
coded_turns.note: Probes the compute-cost extrapolation by checking the core-count assumption behind the wall-clock figures.

coded_turns.quote: Okay. No. Let's go ahead and run it.
coded_turns.note: Approves launching the R=40k committed evidence run rather than deferring to derivation.

coded_turns.quote: starting a new session to create a skill that generates a report on whether the derivation guide is following best practices and to simply flag issues
coded_turns.note: Spins out a side initiative: a derivation-audit skill checking citekeys and externally-validated evidence; asks for a generation prompt.

coded_turns.quote: <summary>Background command "Phase-8 committed evidence run (R=40000, 28 cores)" completed (exit code 0)</summary>
coded_turns.note: Task notification: the R=40k committed evidence run finished, producing the (A)+(C) surface.

coded_turns.quote: <summary>Background command "Re-render derivation.qmd to verify Hens-Aerts citation edit" completed (exit code 0)</summary>
coded_turns.note: Task notification: re-render verifying the Hens-Aerts citation edit completed clean.

coded_turns.quote: I agree with A
coded_turns.note: Picks option A: derive the general-MVN (A)+(C) now rather than buy more expensive N points first.

coded_turns.quote: <summary>Background command "Smoke-test general-MVN (A)+(C) Taylor harness" completed (exit code 0)</summary>
coded_turns.note: Task notification: the Taylor-machinery harness for the general (A)+(C) derivation smoke-passed.

coded_turns.quote: That's fine. But are we up to date on on literature?
coded_turns.note: Checks for missing literature before proceeding; guards against reinventing-the-wheel on the new derivation.

coded_turns.quote: What do you mean by asymptote harness?
coded_turns.note: Asks the AI to define its proposed object before endorsing the (i)-vs-(ii) plan.

coded_turns.quote: <summary>Background command "Re-run asymptote harness (fit_one export fixed)" completed (exit code 0)</summary>
coded_turns.note: Task notification: asymptote harness re-ran after a parLapply export bug fix.

coded_turns.quote: <summary>Background command "Measure Cov(g,delta) to confirm the dropped-covariance diagnosis" completed (exit code 0)</summary>
coded_turns.note: Task notification: covariance-check diagnostic completed, confirming the dropped-covariance diagnosis.

coded_turns.quote: Let's write it up and then commit. Then lets strategize on a closed form solution.
coded_turns.note: Directs write-up + commit of the empirically-validated structure now, with closed-form deferred to a strategy step.

coded_turns.quote: <summary>Background command "Render derivation.qmd to verify Phase-3a write-up" completed (exit code 0)</summary>
coded_turns.note: Task notification: render verifying the Phase-3a write-up completed, gating the commit.

coded_turns.quote: I do want to make sure we aren't reinventing the wheel and that we have searched literature, so do pass that along
coded_turns.note: Orders a context handoff for a new session and insists the literature-search guard carry over into plan mode.

coded_turns.quote: Give me the prompt for the next session
coded_turns.note: Requests the resume prompt to seed the next session's plan mode.

ai_side.evidence: which of (reframe-emphasis / derive the MAR `(A)+(C)` / re-read Claeskens–Consentino + Cavanaugh first) should I tee up next?
ai_side.note: AI puts the next-step choice to Marcus rather than picking silently.

ai_side.evidence: **(a)** proceed to the general-MVN `(A)+(C)` derivation now ... or **(b)** first run extra N (3k/6k) to bracket the asymptote
ai_side.note: AI lays out two options with trade-offs and a stated lean for Marcus to pick.

ai_side.evidence: **R=40k with the current grid (~47 min)** ... Which R do you want me to launch (40k / 20k / 60k, or the trimmed-grid variant)?
ai_side.note: AI presents an R/compute menu with a recommendation and surfaces the launch decision.

ai_side.evidence: Good catch on my part — the first "pass" was a false positive from the pipe.
ai_side.note: AI flags that a prior smoke 'pass' was a false positive and re-ran cleanly.

ai_side.evidence: `term-a-mar-asymptote.R` is flawed — I'll mark it as exploratory/not-evidence (or remove it)
ai_side.note: AI proactively flags a flawed script so it cannot be mistaken for verification evidence.

ai_side.evidence: **Open decisions** (monotone-first?, empirical non-mono OK?, acquire Cox–Snell?)
ai_side.note: AI carries forward explicit unresolved decisions into the handoff rather than resolving them itself.

proposed_codes.name: monitor-background-job
proposed_codes.definition: The human (or the harness on the human's behalf) surfaces a completed background task notification, keeping a long-running compute/verification job under supervision without issuing a new instruction.
proposed_codes.example_quote: <summary>Background command "Phase-8 committed evidence run (R=40000, 28 cores)" completed (exit code 0)</summary>
